---
title: "Business Analytics for Organisations"
---

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| cache: True
#| out.width: "100%"
knitr::include_graphics('image/UoL Zoom Backgrounds_20225.jpg')
```

## Welcome! {.unnumbered}

This module is designed for...

## Key Details {.unnumbered}

Shunya Kimura will be taking **weeks A -- Z**.

(INSERT HEAD SHOT)

**Contact**: s.kimura\@liverpool.ac.uk

Book a meeting (link to Bookings with me)

The module is also supported by XYZ who will be... They offer their own office hours for additional support outside of the lectures/seminars, they’ll let you know when they take place.

Keep an eye on the module announcements page (append link) for further updates and consult Canvas (append link) for all essential module information, including key details about assessment submission etc.

## Timetable {.unnumbered}

To access all recordings of the lectures click here (append link) (you need to go via Canvas).

Links to slides are provided with each week’s materials.

The topics covered over the next X weeks are:

|     | Week Beginning | Theme            | Topic                          |
|-----|----------------|:-----------------|--------------------------------|
| 1   | 26th Jan.      | Section1         | [Week 1]({{< var urls.u01 >}}) |
| 2   |                | Section1         |                                |
| 3   |                | Section1         | [Week 1]({{< var urls.u01 >}}) |
| 4   |                | Section1         |                                |
| 5   |                | Section1         | [Week 1]({{< var urls.u01 >}}) |
|     |                | **Reading week** | **Reading week**               |
| 6   |                | Section1         | Coming soon...                 |

## Assessments {.unnumbered}

The assessment is... word counts... deadlines...

Please include the word count at the top of the essay and submit your essay using your candidate number as the filename. Please check the Management School essay submission checklist and penalties for late submission and exceeding word limits. You will find useful guidance for writing and presenting essays on the Management School student website. These guidelines are designed to help you, and you should read them carefully and do your best to follow them. Good essays give clear and focused answers to the question asked, they have clear structures, and they will be adequately and appropriately referenced. They do not provide a vague and unstructured discussion of the topic.

Plagiarism is taken extremely seriously and can disqualify you from the module (for details of what constitutes plagiarism see ...).  If you are in doubt about any of this, ask us. This is a reminder that there are also very clear guidelines on the use of AI. See here (append some link).

Click here to see two past assessment examples (append link). One scored in the high 70s, the other in the mid 60s. I will talk through these in class.

## Help & Support {.unnumbered}

For specific assistance with this module, you can:

-   Refer to the Canvas (append link) assessment tab for queries about module assessments.

-   Ask a question at the end of lectures or during the computer practicals.

-   Book into the Office Hours (append link).

## Acknowledgements {.unnumbered}

This workbook is created using the [Quarto](https://quarto.org/) publishing system. Elements of this workbook are partially based on and modified from:

-   INSERT ANY MATERIAL HERE

This year's workbook takes inspiration and design elements from:

-   The [GEOG0030: Geocomputation 2023-2024](https://jtvandijk.github.io/GEOG0030_20232024/) workbook by [Justin van Dijk](https://www.mappingdutchman.com)
